subnets.name: Compute Horde
subnets.description: computehorde is a specialized subnet (subnet 12) within the Bittensor network, designed to provide scalable, decentralized, and trusted GPU computing power for validators across other Bittensor subnets. By transforming untrusted GPUs from miners into reliable compute resources, computehorde reduces dependency on centralized services and enables cost-effective, large-scale validation. Key features include fair and verified work mechanisms, scalable mining with multiple executors per miner, hardware class-based resource allocation, a developer SDK for easy integration, collateral-based trust for organic jobs, and an optional DDoS shield for miners. The project aims to support a wide range of GPU types and offers a robust scoring and incentive system to ensure quality and accountability.

subnets.name: Team TBC
subnets.description: Nodexo is a decentralized AI compute platform that connects users needing GPU resources for AI workloads with providers offering their hardware, enabling scalable, on-demand access to high-performance GPUs at transparent, usage-based rates. Powered by the Bittensor blockchain (specifically subnet 27), Nodexo allows individuals and organizations to rent or supply GPU compute for tasks such as model training and inference, with automated provisioning, secure API access, and enterprise-grade reliability. Providers earn rewards based on performance, while validators ensure resource integrity and allocation. The platform supports instant deployment, open-source tools, and aims to make AI compute more affordable and accessible compared to traditional cloud providers.

subnets.name: Coldint
subnets.description: Coldint is a collaborative, distributed, and incentivized training subnet on the Bittensor network (subnet 29) focused on advancing state-of-the-art machine learning model training and research. The project incentivizes miners to contribute improved models, code, and insights, fostering both individual and collective progress. Rewards are given for model performance improvements, code contributions, bug fixes, and valuable suggestions, with an emphasis on transparency, fairness, and open sharing of results. Coldint aims to accelerate innovation by enabling continuous, high-quality iterations and combining individual efforts into superior collective models, all while maintaining public leaderboards and open-source codebases.

subnets.name: Endure Network
subnets.description: The risk intelligence network

subnets.name: Recall
subnets.description: Recall provides decentralized retrieval-augmented generation to Bittensor. Miners serve embedding models, vector search, and LLM inference. Validators independently evaluate retrieval accuracy and answer quality. The subnet discovers the best RAG pipeline through open competition and routes user queries to the top performers. Think of it as an always-improving, community-owned search engine with citations.

subnets.name: ItsAI
subnets.description: ItsAI is a bittensor subnet focused on high-quality AI detection for texts. Recognised as the most accurate AI detector by MGTD benchmark.

subnets.name: ReadyAI
subnets.description: ReadyAI is an open-source project that provides a low-cost, resource-efficient data structuring and semantic tagging pipeline designed for individuals and businesses to convert raw data into structured, AI-ready formats. Built on the Bittensor ecosystem, ReadyAI leverages distributed miners and validators to process and annotate data using large language models (LLMs) such as OpenAI's GPT-4o, Anthropic, and others. The system incentivizes data contribution and validation through a scoring mechanism based on cosine similarity, ensuring data integrity and quality. ReadyAI is optimized for integration with vector databases and AI applications, making it scalable and accessible for a wide range of data processing needs.

subnets.name: BitMind
subnets.description: Gas (Generative Adversarial Subnet) is a Bittensor subnet designed to advance synthetic media detection and generation through adversarial competition. Inspired by generative adversarial networks (GANs), it features two parallel tracks: discriminative mining, where participants submit AI models to detect fake images, videos, and audio, and generative mining, where participants generate synthetic media to challenge detectors. All models are evaluated in the cloud, lowering hardware requirements and encouraging innovation. The system incentivizes continual improvement by rewarding both effective detectors and convincing generators, ensuring rapid evolution of detection tools to keep pace with emerging synthetic media threats.

subnets.name: OxMarkets

subnets.name: Chunking
subnets.description: The project provides a blockchain platform focused on validator and miner participation, offering interactive benchmarking tools to assess performance. It tracks metrics such as chunks produced, tokens processed, recall, and precision, and compares its performance relative to competitors and other AI-based solutions. The platform emphasizes transparency through dashboards and detailed statistics, catering to users interested in network performance and operational analytics.

subnets.name: Almanac
subnets.description: Incentivized market intelligence.

subnets.name: Graphite
subnets.description: GraphiteAI is a decentralized network built on Bittensor, specializing in solving complex graph optimization problems such as the Traveling Salesman Problem (TSP). By leveraging decentralized intelligence and scalable computational power, Graphite connects miners and validators to efficiently process and solve NP-hard graph challenges. The platform supports multiple input methods and provides a suite of algorithms, including heuristics and deep learning-based approaches, while incentivizing miners to develop high-performance solvers through a reward mechanism. GraphiteAI aims to democratize access to advanced graph solutions, making them accessible to both individuals and organizations through an intuitive interface and comprehensive support.

subnets.name: Score
subnets.description: Making every camera intelligent

subnets.name: Talisman AI
subnets.description: Talisman AI (Subnet 45) is an AI-powered financial reasoning agent designed to provide real-time trading insights for the Bittensor ecosystem. It continuously analyzes social media sentiment, on-chain activity, and market data to surface actionable signals and explain them in plain language. The system consists of miners that collect and analyze relevant data, validators that ensure data quality, and wallet integration for delivering personalized trading or staking recommendations. The project aims to empower users with situational awareness and decision support for crypto trading, with a phased roadmap expanding from social sentiment analysis to comprehensive on-chain and market intelligence.

subnets.name: Dojo
subnets.description: dojo v2 is a decentralized generative adversarial network (GAN) platform built on Bittensor, designed to foster competitive AI model development through zero-sum incentives. Unlike traditional GANs, dojo v2 challenges miners to produce outputs that are not only indistinguishable from high-quality baselines but also superior, creating a dynamic environment where participants act as both generators and discriminators. Miners can easily participate without running servers by registering and using a browser wallet, while validators help maintain network integrity. The project emphasizes ease of entry, competitive AI advancement, and decentralized participation.

subnets.name: EfficientFrontier
subnets.description: Efficient Frontier is a collaborative project by SignalPlus and Bittensor aimed at identifying optimal, risk-weighted crypto trading strategies by integrating decentralized machine learning networks with live trading data. Leveraging Bittensor's blockchain-powered AI protocol, the platform enables users to access professional-grade trading tools and democratizes strategy development and evaluation. Strategies are ranked using a transparent, risk-adjusted scoring system that emphasizes consistent returns, minimal drawdowns, and real trading performance, with daily scores and rewards distributed based on recent results and adherence to platform rules.

subnets.name: oneoneone
subnets.description: oneoneone (subnet 111) is a decentralized AI-powered protocol on the Bittensor network focused on collecting, validating, and distributing high-quality user-generated content from platforms like Google Maps, Yelp, forums, and social media. The network leverages miners to scrape and structure data, validators to ensure authenticity and quality through synthetic challenges, and provides API access for developers and businesses via a subscription model. Key features include AI-driven sentiment and intent analysis, multi-language support, real-time validation, and a profit-sharing mechanism for network participants.

subnets.name: MANTIS
subnets.description: Mantis Bittensor Subnet 123 is a decentralized signal processing and prediction platform where validators sample, decrypt, and score miner-submitted payloads related to financial market challenges. The system supports multiple challenge types, including binary direction prediction, volatility regime detection, breakout events, and cross-sectional asset ranking. Validators use advanced logistic regression-based scoring, sybil resistance mechanisms, and dual-path encryption to ensure fair, secure, and private evaluation of predictions. Results are aggregated, smoothed, and set as on-chain weights, incentivizing high-quality, unique signal contributions. The architecture leverages SQLite for efficient storage and supports modular challenge definitions and scoring logic.

subnets.name: Swarm
subnets.description: Swarm is an open benchmark platform for training and evaluating AI models in autonomous drone navigation. It challenges models to pilot drones through 1,000 procedurally generated 3D environments using only a depth camera and flight state, with no access to maps, GPS, or prior knowledge. Models are scored on landing success, speed, and safety, with results published on a public leaderboard. Swarm is powered by Bittensor (subnet 124) and supports both simulation and real-world deployment, including open-source drone hardware. The project aims to standardize and democratize AI flight policy evaluation, fostering open innovation in autonomous aerial robotics.
